title: Courseforge JSON-LD Page Metadata v1
description: Formalizes the JSON-LD contract emitted by Courseforge on every course-module HTML page inside <script type="application/ld+json"> blocks. Top-level type is CourseModule. Consumed by Trainforge's html_content_parser and process_course pipeline as the authoritative metadata source (priority: JSON-LD > data-cf-* > regex heuristics). Per REC-JSL-01 this schema tightens the misconception shape (both keys required) and normalizes bloomRange to an array. Wave 9 adds optional page-level and section-level sourceReferences[] (each conforming to schemas/knowledge/source_reference.schema.json).
$comment: Shape lifted from Courseforge/scripts/generate_course.py: _build_page_metadata (L571-595), _build_objectives_metadata (L512-546), _build_sections_metadata (L549-568). Taxonomy $refs target Worker F's schemas (merged in PR #20). Enum reconciliation: learningObjectives[].assessmentSuggestions and suggestedAssessmentTypes both reference question_type.json's 9-value QuestionType union; emit-side vocabulary at :534-539 and :689,707 is a subset. Per KG-quality review Wave 1.2 plan, Worker C outline at plans/kg-quality-review-2026-04/discovery/c-jsonld-contract.md. Wave 9 source-provenance additions: optional sourceReferences arrays at page and section level, $ref-ing the canonical SourceReference shape.

@context.description: JSON-LD context namespace for Courseforge CourseModule emits.

weekNumber.description: Week index for the page. Week 0 reserved for course-entry / syllabus pages.

moduleType.description: Weekly module page type: overview, content, application, assessment, summary, or discussion. References Worker F's module_type taxonomy.

pageId.description: Page identifier unique within the course. Multiple templates exist (week_XX_overview, week_XX_content_NN_<slug>, etc.) so no single pattern is enforced.

learningObjectives.description: Structured learning objectives shown on the page. Elided when empty.

sections.description: Page section metadata. Elided when empty.

misconceptions.description: Common misconceptions with corrections. Per REC-JSL-01 both misconception and correction keys are required.

suggestedAssessmentTypes.description: Page-level recommended question formats. References Worker F's question_type union.

prerequisitePages.description: pageIds that are prerequisite to this page. Consume-only today (Trainforge reads at html_content_parser.py:219; no emit site yet). Reserved here for Wave 2 REC-JSL-02 emit.

sourceReferences.description: Wave 9: optional page-level source attribution. Each entry references a DART-synthesized source block (canonical shape: schemas/knowledge/source_reference.schema.json). Emitted by generate_course.py when source_module_map.json is populated; absent when no DART input (course_generation workflow) or when routing produced no mapping. Elided when empty so legacy callers stay schema-clean.

bloomDistribution.description: Wave 61: aggregate Bloom/cognitive-domain distribution across this page's learningObjectives. Emitted iff at least one LO has a populated bloomLevel. Lets KG consumers answer 'what's the cognitive-demand profile of this page/course?' without re-aggregating per-LO data.

blocks.description: Phase 2: stable in-memory Block intermediate; canonical projection of learningObjectives[]/sections[]/misconceptions[]. Optional and additive — legacy emits without blocks[] still validate. The redundancy with the older arrays is intentional during the Phase 2 migration window; a follow-up wave will collapse the older arrays once consumers prefer blocks[].

provenance.description: Phase 2: per-page run-level provenance metadata. Optional — emitted when the renderer has a populated runId.

tiers.description: Per-tier provider/model attribution for this run. Mirrors the Touch.tier enum.

contentHash.description: Phase 2: sha256 hex of the canonical Block payload list for re-execution drift detection. Same content -> same hash regardless of which tier authored the page.

$comment: Phase 2 migration window: blocks[] is the stable intermediate projection of learningObjectives[]/sections[]/misconceptions[]. The redundancy is intentional and will be collapsed in a follow-up wave once Trainforge consumers prefer blocks[].

bloomLevel.description: Bloom's taxonomy level. May be null when detect_bloom_level fails; downstream chunks treat null as authoritative (see KG-quality review gap #14). Wave 58: when the LO text contains multiple canonical verbs (e.g., 'Analyze and evaluate X') this field carries the primary (highest-priority) match; the full set lives in bloomLevels[].

bloomVerb.description: Detected Bloom's verb (free string). Wave 58: primary verb only; multi-verb LOs also emit bloomVerbs[] in canonical iteration order (bloomVerbs[0] == bloomVerb).

bloomLevels.description: Wave 58: every Bloom's level detected in the LO statement, in canonical iteration order (longest-verb-first, higher-level ties winning). bloomLevels[0] equals bloomLevel. Elided when no verb matches. Level enum values may repeat if multiple verbs on the same level appear; dedup is caller's responsibility.

bloomVerbs.description: Wave 58: every canonical Bloom's verb detected in the LO statement, in the same iteration order as bloomLevels. bloomVerbs[0] equals bloomVerb. Each verb appears at most once (whole-word deduplication). Elided when no verb matches.

cognitiveDomain.description: Revised Bloom's knowledge dimension. Defaults to 'conceptual' when unknown.

keyConcepts.description: Concept slugs associated with this objective.

targetedConcepts.description: Wave 57: Bloom-qualified LO→concept edges. Each entry binds a canonical concept slug to the cognitive demand at which this LO targets it (inherited from the parent LO's bloomLevel). Emitted only when both keyConcepts and bloomLevel are populated. Enables KG consumers to materialize LO--[bloomLevel]-->concept edges directly rather than inferring from chunk co-occurrence. Concept slugs here are a subset of keyConcepts; the two stay in sync by construction.

bloomLevel.description: Bloom's taxonomy level at which this LO targets the concept. Inherited from the parent LO at emit time.

assessmentSuggestions.description: Suggested question formats for this objective. References Worker F's question_type union (9 values; emitter uses a 5-value subset).

prerequisiteObjectives.description: LO IDs that are prerequisite to this objective. Emitted at generate_course.py:542-544; not currently consumed at chunk granularity (see KG-quality review gap #2).

hierarchyLevel.description: Wave 59: LO hierarchy tier derived from the canonical ID prefix. TO-NN → 'terminal' (course-wide rollups); CO-NN → 'chapter' (chapter-level LOs that roll up to a terminal). Lets KG consumers filter by tier without re-parsing the ID pattern.

parentObjectiveId.description: Wave 59: Parent LO ID — the terminal objective a chapter LO rolls up to. Absent on terminal LOs (they are KG roots). Optional on chapter LOs — emitted when upstream supplies the mapping via the LO dict's 'parent_objective_id'/'parentObjectiveId' field (e.g., from synthesized_objectives.json); elided when no parent is known. Heuristic inference from key-concept overlap is deferred to a later wave.

abcd.description: Phase 6: ABCD framework discrete fields (Audience, Behavior, Condition, Degree). Optional for legacy LOs that pre-date Phase 6 (preserves backward compatibility with existing synthesized_objectives.json fixtures); required emit-side for newly authored LOs once the course-outliner agent rolls forward to ABCD-aware output. Phase 7+ will promote this field to LearningObjective.required once corpus calibration confirms safe.

AbcdObjective.description: Phase 6: discrete ABCD-framework fields for a learning objective. ABCD = Audience (who), Behavior (observable action: verb + action_object), Condition (under what circumstances), Degree (level of mastery / accuracy). Composed back into prose by lib.ontology.learning_objectives.compose_abcd_prose. The split lets the course-outliner agent emit constrained-decoding-validated structured output and lets AbcdObjectiveValidator gate that the behavior.verb is consistent with the LO's bloomLevel via BLOOMS_VERBS lookup.

audience.description: ABCD 'A': who the learner is (e.g., 'Students', 'First-year nursing students').

behavior.description: ABCD 'B': the observable action the learner will perform. Split into a Bloom-aligned verb and the noun-phrase object the verb acts on so AbcdObjectiveValidator can check verb-Bloom alignment without re-parsing prose.

verb.description: Bloom-aligned action verb (e.g., 'identify', 'analyze', 'evaluate'). Validated against BLOOMS_VERBS[lo.bloomLevel] by lib.validators.abcd_objective.AbcdObjectiveValidator.

action_object.description: Noun phrase the verb acts on (e.g., 'the parts of a cell', 'differential equations of order n').

condition.description: ABCD 'C': circumstances under which the behavior is performed (e.g., 'from a labeled diagram', 'given a closed-book exam'). May be empty when the LO carries no specific condition.

degree.description: ABCD 'D': level of mastery / accuracy (e.g., 'with 90% accuracy', 'within 5 minutes'). May be empty when the LO carries no specific degree.

contentType.description: Section content type (8-value enum from Worker F's content_type taxonomy: definition, example, procedure, comparison, exercise, overview, summary, explanation).

bloomRange.description: Bloom's level range for the section. Per REC-JSL-01 normalized to array (emitter at generate_course.py:566 wraps scalar strings). Legacy str|array drift handled defensively at consume side but emit is array-only.

sourceReferences.description: Wave 9: optional section-level source attribution. Emit only when a section draws from a different set of source blocks than the page overall (override pattern). Falls back to page-level sourceReferences when absent. Each entry conforms to schemas/knowledge/source_reference.schema.json.

BloomDistribution.description: Wave 61: Aggregate Bloom/cognitive-domain distribution across a page's learningObjectives. Counts the primary (singular) bloomLevel of each LO — multi-level LOs (via bloomLevels[]) are NOT double-counted here. Consumers wanting the richer multi-level aggregate should union the per-LO bloomLevels[] arrays themselves.

total.description: Number of LOs on this page with a non-null bloomLevel. LOs with null bloomLevel are skipped entirely.

byLevel.description: Per-Bloom-level LO counts. Keys are canonical BloomLevel enum members; zero-count levels are elided for payload terseness.

byCognitiveDomain.description: Per-cognitive-domain LO counts, derived from bloomLevel via lib.ontology.bloom.bloom_to_cognitive_domain. Zero-count domains elided. Emitted alongside byLevel when at least one LO's bloomLevel maps to a canonical domain.

Misconception.description: Per REC-JSL-01: both 'misconception' and 'correction' keys required. Prevents the silent correction-edge drop flagged in KG-quality review gap #2. Wave 60: optional bloomLevel + cognitiveDomain fields let KG consumers filter misconceptions by cognitive demand (e.g., 'apply'-level mistakes differ from 'analyze'-level ones in the diagnostic questions they warrant).

bloomLevel.description: Wave 60: Bloom's taxonomy level for the misconception. Inferred at emit time from the correction statement via detect_bloom_level, with fallback to the misconception statement. Upstream-supplied values (on the input dict under 'bloomLevel' / 'bloom_level') override detection. Null when no canonical verb matches either statement; elided entirely from the emit when null so legacy consumers don't see a surprise field.

cognitiveDomain.description: Wave 60: knowledge domain derived from bloomLevel via lib.ontology.bloom.bloom_to_cognitive_domain. Emitted iff bloomLevel is populated.

Touch.description: Phase 2: one revision attribution event in a Block's touch chain. Each tier (outline / validation / rewrite) emits a Touch when it modifies the block. Companion to Courseforge/scripts/blocks.py::Touch. The chain is cumulative — full retention per Phase 2 pre-resolved decision #2.

model.description: LLM model identifier that authored the touch (e.g., 'qwen2.5-14b-instruct-q4_K_M', 'claude-sonnet-4-6').

provider.description: Provider backend that produced the touch. 'deterministic' covers Phase-1 deterministic templating paths.

tier.description: Phase 3 / 3.5 tier that produced the touch. outline = first-pass draft; validation = validator-driven retouch (legacy); rewrite = budget-exhausted escalation; outline_val = post-outline inter-tier validator pass/fail signal (Phase 3.5 Subtask 17); rewrite_val = post-rewrite validator pass/fail signal (Phase 3.5 Subtask 13).

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp at which the touch was emitted.

decisionCaptureId.description: Wave 112 invariant: '{capture_file_basename}:{event_index}' pointing into the JSONL written by _emit_decision. Non-empty by construction.

Block.description: Phase 2: canonical intermediate block. Companion to Courseforge/scripts/blocks.py::Block. Stable in-memory representation between the Phase-1 ContentGeneratorProvider (str HTML today) and the legacy renderer surface; Phase 2 widens the provider to return Block instances. Phase 3 adds validationAttempts + escalationMarker for the per-block regeneration-budget primitive.

blockId.description: Position-based ID per Phase 2 pre-resolved decision #1: '{page_id}#{block_type}_{slug}_{idx}'.

blockType.description: One of the 16 canonical Phase 2 block types (mirror of Courseforge/scripts/blocks.py::BLOCK_TYPES).

sequence.description: 1-indexed (or 0-indexed; renderer-specific) position within the page. Stable within a render and used to disambiguate same-type blocks.

contentHash.description: SHA-256 hex of the canonical Block payload. Excludes touched_by, sequence, validation_attempts, escalation_marker so a touch- or budget-only revision keeps a stable hash.

touchedBy.description: Cumulative touch chain — every revision is appended. Bounded ~12k entries × ~80 bytes ≈ ~1 MB JSON before gzip per course; well within IMSCC payload budgets.

pageId.description: Page identifier the block belongs to. Same shape as the top-level pageId.

bloomLevel.description: Bloom's taxonomy level for the block (when applicable to the block_type).

contentTypeLabel.description: Section content classification (mirrors data-cf-content-type emit; 8-value enum from content_type taxonomy when applicable).

objectiveIds.description: Canonical LO IDs (TO-NN / CO-NN) the block teaches or assesses.

sourceIds.description: DART sourceId(s) that ground this block (shape: 'dart:{slug}#{block_id}').

sourcePrimary.description: The primary sourceId for the block when one source dominates (subset of sourceIds).

templateType.description: Template variant name when the block is rendered by a templated component.

keyTerms.description: Slugified key terms attached to the block (mirrors data-cf-key-terms).

validationAttempts.description: Phase-3 regeneration-budget counter; incremented per failed validator pass on the outline tier.

escalationMarker.description: Phase-3 escalation tag; set to a non-null marker when the block was escalated to the rewrite tier.
